description: Harness bootstrap can complete and leave initializer marker
evidence: Verified 2026-03-04: bootstrap runs clean, all artifacts exist (init.sh: 2312 bytes, feature_list.json: 7636 bytes, claude-progress.txt: 1538 bytes), .agent/runtime/initializer.done contains 'done 2026-03-04T17:34:46'

description: Single cycle run works and writes logs
evidence: Verified 2026-03-04: harness.py correctly writes cycle logs. .agent/logs contains agent_output_cycle_XXXXXX.log and agent_error_cycle_XXXXXX.log files. .agent/runtime/harness.log shows 'cycle start cycle_XXXXXX feature_id=YYYY' and 'cycle end cycle_XXXXXX rc=N' entries. Multiple cycles (1-5) executed successfully with proper logging.

description: Run-forever mode can continuously execute cycles
steps: Observe multiple cycle logs over time
Stop process manually and verify clean restart
evidence: Skipped by user instruction on 2026-03-04 to unblock downstream progress. Historical failure context retained in .agent/runtime/harness.log with repeated rc=1 cycles for feature_id=M0-003.

description: ReAct loop includes explicit think-act-observe traces
steps: Task execution includes reasoning before tool call
Tool call results are observed and used
Agent emits final answer or next action
evidence: Verified 2026-03-04: harness.py run_cycle() now calls extract_react_traces() and log_react_traces() after agent execution. Traces logged to .agent/runtime/react_traces.jsonl with type (think/observe/final_answer), cycle, seq, at timestamp, and content. Coding prompt instructs ## Think/## Observe/## Final Answer blocks. Verification test confirmed all trace types extracted and logged correctly.

description: Core tools available: file, shell, web
evidence: Verified 2026-03-04: Created tools/ package with FileTool, ShellTool, WebTool. FileTool supports read/write/list/delete/exists operations. ShellTool executes commands with blocked pattern safety (rm -rf /, fork bombs, etc.). WebTool fetches HTTP/HTTPS content with URL validation. ToolRegistry provides unified interface and schema export. Verification test passed: file read/write/delete works, shell echo/pwd works and dangerous commands blocked, web fetch to httpbin.org works, invalid URL schemes rejected.

description: Tool schema is strict and validated
evidence: Verified 2026-03-04: Implemented SchemaValidator class in tools/base.py with full JSON Schema-like validation (type, enum, min/max, minLength/maxLength). All three tools (file, shell, web) have valid schemas with properties and required fields. Validation tests: missing required params rejected, wrong types rejected, invalid enum values rejected, multiple errors reported clearly in single message. ToolRegistry.execute validates args before dispatch.

description: Tool failure retry and fallback policy
evidence: Verified 2026-03-04: Created tools/retry.py with comprehensive retry infrastructure. RetryPolicy detects transient errors (timeouts, HTTP 5xx, rate limits, file locks) via TransientErrorPattern. RetryExecutor implements exponential backoff with jitter and max delay cap. FallbackHandler provides tool-specific fallbacks (file encoding alternatives, web extended timeout). RetryAttempt and RetryLog dataclasses track all attempts with timestamps. Audit logging writes JSONL entries with type=retry_log. ToolRegistry.set_retry_executor() and execute_with_retry() provide integration. Verification test passed all 10 checks: transient detection, retry behavior, non-transient skip, fallback invocation, audit logging, registry integration, pattern matching, exponential backoff, and default handlers.

description: Constitutional safety policy integrated
steps: Apply policy checks before risky actions
Return refusal or safe alternative when needed
evidence: Verified 2026-03-04: Created safety/ package with Constitution, PolicyRule, PolicyChecker classes. Machine-readable policy rules in safety/policies/default.json (13 rules covering shell, file, web tools). PolicyChecker.check() returns PolicyDecision with allowed, action, reason, alternative, risk_level. ToolRegistry.set_policy_checker() integrates policy checks - blocked actions return ToolResult(success=False, error='BLOCKED by policy: ...'). Audit logging supported via audit_log_path. Verification test passed all 9 checks: constitution loading, JSON serialization, shell blocking, file access blocking, URL scheme blocking, registry integration, safe commands pass, audit logging.

description: Risk classification for user requests
evidence: Verified 2026-03-04: Created safety/classifier.py with RequestClassifier, RequestDecision, RiskCategory, RiskPattern classes. Implemented 9 default risk patterns covering: system destruction (CRITICAL), data exfiltration (CRITICAL), credential access (CRITICAL), security bypass (HIGH), privilege escalation (HIGH), network attacks (HIGH), arbitrary execution (HIGH), sensitive access (MEDIUM), unsafe operations (MEDIUM). RequestClassifier.classify() returns RequestDecision with risk_level, allowed, matched_patterns, reasons, requires_confirmation. CRITICAL always blocked, HIGH blocked without confirmation handler or calls on_confirm callback. Audit logging via audit_log_path parameter writes JSON lines with type=request_classification. Verification test passed all 10 checks: imports, low/medium/high/critical classification, gating behavior, confirmation callback, audit logging, gate_request(), get_risk_summary().
